name: Enterprise Bank & Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business banking, wealth management, and financial education through Enterprise University.
description_long:
Enterprise Bank & Trust is a full-service bank positioned as a financial partner for closely-held companies and individuals seeking lifetime financial success. The bank operates multiple branch locations, including recent expansions in Kansas City and Arizona through branch acquisitions, and maintains FDIC insurance backing all deposits with the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Enterprise offers a comprehensive suite of banking services designed to serve both personal and business customers, with particular emphasis on supporting entrepreneurial ventures and small business growth.

The bank's service offerings include online banking, credit card products, personal and business banking accounts, wealth management services through partnerships with eMoney and Fidelity, and educational programs. Enterprise Bank & Trust provides traditional banking products such as checking and savings accounts, while also offering specialized business solutions. The institution emphasizes fraud prevention and security, explicitly warning customers that they will never request passwords or two-factor authentication codes via unsolicited contact.

Enterprise distinguishes itself through Enterprise University, a no-cost skill development program offering live, interactive webinars on finance, operations, leadership, human resources, and sales—positioning the bank as an educational partner rather than just a financial service provider. The company also emphasizes community impact with dedicated community partnerships programs and publishes annual reports documenting their community commitment. Their Enterprise.ing Podcast features conversations with business leaders about building professional connections, adding a thought leadership component.

Enterprise Bank & Trust appears to be a mid-to-regional bank with physical branch presence and digital banking capabilities. However, the website content provided does not include specific information about interest rates, fees, loan terms, minimum balances, or competitive metrics that would allow a comprehensive assessment against other banking institutions. The recent branch acquisitions suggest growth and expansion, but limited detail is provided about the scope of services available or specializations beyond general business and personal banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking portal with multi-account access
Client portal access for account management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Enterprise University offers no-cost professional development courses and webinars for business customers
Dual wealth management platform access through eMoney and Fidelity partnerships
Multi-channel access including online banking, mobile, and physical branch locations
Strong fraud prevention messaging and security practices protecting customer information
Community-focused with dedicated community partnership programs and annual impact reporting
Recent expansion through branch acquisitions in Kansas City and Arizona regions
cons: Website content does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, or fee structures for any products
Limited information about minimum balance requirements, account types, or product details
No transparent pricing information for credit cards, loans, or wealth management services
best_for: Small business owners and closely-held companies seeking integrated banking and financial education
Entrepreneurs interested in professional development through no-cost business courses
Customers in Kansas City and Arizona regions with local branch access needs
diagnosis: Enterprise Bank & Trust is best suited for small business owners and entrepreneurs in its geographic footprint who value educational partnerships alongside traditional banking services. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparent disclosure of rates, fees, and specific product terms needed to evaluate whether their offerings are competitive compared to other regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
